item.title: RIP imgur Cripple
item.description:
Sad story warning! This post is long and long overdue. By now, some of you may have heard that @DarwinFinch, imgur's resident cripple, aka Holly with the MD, passed away several months ago. I'm her husband Luke, and I'd been living with and caring for Holly full-time for almost nine years. You might have seen us pop up on imgur from time-to-time, as Holly had always been embraced by a huge number of (I'm hoping we still use this term) imgurians.
Understandably, posting to imgur wasn't my first reaction to Holly's passing, but a rare IG/imgur hybrid user mentioned it in a recent post. It's long overdue to engage with the community that first boosted Holly's profile and sent me on this unbelievable path in the first place. Holly was barely able to check her phone most days during her final 12 months, so posting online became rare altogether. So...
A quick recap. Holly was a neuroscientist from Australia studying her PhD. We met at the start of 2015, and basically had a third date that never ended. She was teaching at the local uni, I was bumming around in a film & TV course, having just come back all cashed up from the mines. We moved in together October 2015 (right around Holly's birthday), and I'd been her full-time, government-pensioned carer ever since. This was the first time Holly had ever lived out of home (a feat she had thought impossible) and we had massive support from our family and friends. Unfortunately, Holly's LGMD was still degenerative, and by late 2016 she had to leave her job and PhD studies. It was around this time that she'd decided to document her failing health online (among you fine people), and eventually became an advocate for euthanasia in our home state of Queensland.
We survived a house move, two cats in a tiny apartment (the tax is coming), and many bad, terrible, shitty days in between. Holly's profile boosted from a handful of feature articles in magazines and online. During 2020, Holly's anxiety peaked. She handed in her angry feminist card and asked me if I would marry her - another feat she swore impossible. We were wed in early 2021 in our home at the time, exactly six years after our third date (when we'd decided we were exclusive-official). Our parents and a carer attended. We dressed up a little, didn't spend a lot. Holly's dress was $40 online (her custom Alice In Wonderland bouquet cost more!), and I had a haircut and a cheap suit tailored. The celebrant was a family friend who only charged us the court fees, and COVID and Holly's condition meant no reception, no fancy do; just some bubbly and a joint. Get outta my personal space.
I've tried several times, and I cannot describe why or how much I love this photo of Holly.
In late 2021, our landlord decided to sell, and we had a little over two months to vacate. Times was tough all over, but two pensioners in the middle of a heightened COVID housing market had next to no chance. Fortunately, my parents had a workshed/granny flat in disrepair. We talked it over, spoke with my parents, and pulled the trigger. My dear old dad and I (mostly him) spent the next six weeks renovating and extending the flat to take up the entire workshed. It worked out to be far, far cheaper to pay for materials than a year's rent. Fortunately, again, my father is an old tradesperson and general handyman. We managed to construct this all by ourselves. From concept to finished product, and several hardware store visits in between. Holly was ecstatic - she now had a large, open-plan bathroom AND she could choose the colour of her bedroom! It was pink. We finished and, with help from our families, moved in right before Christmas. Just in time to welcome a new addition to the household...
Tedrick Lamar Beardrick Warmos (known to everyone except me as Teddy Bear - don't tell Holly I changed his name) was Holly's dream cat. Some of you fine folks may have even contributed to his acquisition. I put up a crowdfund for Holly's birthday and everyone smashed it. He was named after my recently deceased grandfather, and came to us a couple days after we moved in. Going from a three bedroom duplex to a one bed flat with THREE cats was a massive downsize, but we eventually got a handle on the organised chaos. Yes, I know; adopt, don't shop. But our other two cats were rescues (now royalty) and Holly was putting her metaphorical foot down on this one. One last kitten. Teddy was coming home with us! He's been a sweet, beautiful, hilarious companion, and I don't regret getting him for a second. But now I'm a single, nearly middle aged man and three cats is more than enough for me.
Fast forward a couple more years of long, sleep-filled days. Holly was growing physically weaker, and mentally checked out. For pain and symptom management, she took a combination of medications; pills, oils, and cannabis (all prescribed). She was asleep for 15-18 hours every day, back to eating very little, and generally feeling down and unwell. On Sunday 24th of March Holly developed a cough. She didn't usually pick up bugs, even if I'm coughing and spluttering all over her. This was different. Monday was a lot of coughing and wheezing, and by midday Tuesday, Holly was gasping for me to call an ambulance. She was having trouble breathing. She would tell me later through a BiPAP machine that she felt like she was slowly drowning.
Holly was in hospital a total of two days. After a few hours with a breathing mask on and an oxygen line in her nose, the doctors switched her to a BiPAP machine to aid her breathing. Holly was terrified of the mask, as it both inhaled and exhaled for her like a ventilator. She was unable to sleep during her admission, only managing a couple of hours around 6am on the Wednesday. The medical staff were also concerned that Holly's heavy painkillers would suppress her chest and lung muscles, so they were limiting some of her medications. Wednesday morning she'd been moved from Nambour's ICU to the respiratory ward at Sunshine Coast University Hospital.
Holly hated hospitals. She was exhausted and scared. She just wanted to go home, smoke some pot, and get her back rubbed. I did what I could within the confines of a hospital room, but it was clear that she was in agony. On Wednesday night Holly's parents returned from a trip overseas and came straight to the hospital to be with her while I slept in a companion chair. When they left in the morning to try to get some sleep, Holly's vitals had been steadily improving. At this point, I was still well inside the 'we'll be home by the end of the week' bubble. Holly was not as optimistic.
Thursday. Holly's symptoms were improving, but she was still having difficulty breathing. The doctors told us that the numbers were good and it would be a case of waiting for her lungs to clean themselves. When we were alone, Holly told me she still felt like she was drowning. Every breath was laboured and aided by the machine. The mask had rubbed a wound into the bridge of her nose. Looking back now, I wonder how long she knew that this was the end. Tuesday morning? Wednesday afternoon? It's clear she'd made the decision long before she said it out loud, but she held on as long as she could stand it. Just before midday on the 28th of March, Holly asked me to request end of life treatment from her medical team. This had been an option for around 36 hours, but I'd been urging Holly to hold on a little longer, encouraged by the 'good' numbers. I knew she was serious. She went from a scared mess to bizarrely calm as she doubled down on her wishes. My first reaction was denial, followed by the hardest cry of my adult life in the hospital bathroom. This is exactly what I signed up for, I just never thought it would come so soon. We're old pros at hospitals by now so we spoke to the doctor and began the slow process BEFORE we contacted her family. Holly was able to spend her last few hours surrounded by her loved ones. We laughed and cried. We shared happy moments and tough ones. We hugged and kissed, not sure when our last chance would come. Towards the end of our time together, Holly reassured us all by stating that she was 32 and had never had her heart broken. We all took a few seconds to share some private final words as each of us hugged her one last time. The medical team declared Holly dead at 3:45pm. The death cocktail had its work cut out for it. As the nurses would pump her arms full of morphine and whatever else, Holly would fall asleep, we'd all silently cry, then she'd wake, look around, and go back to sleep. This happened a couple of times. Cause of death was Limb Girdle Muscular Dystrophy (r3/2d, for those wondering) and complications from rhinovirus. Put simply: the common cold.
The first month after Holly was a blur. It still is, but the first month felt surreal. Things have calmed right down now but some days I still catch myself talking to her, or forgetting that she isn't waiting for me at home. I would feel anxious all day, waiting for Holly to yell out or press her call button from the next room. Then 5pm would roll around (Holly's latest wake up time) and I would be lost on what to do with myself. I kept thinking that I was forgetting her medications, or that she was in pain because I hadn't been home in four hours and she'd need to be rolled on her side. Five months later, and I've mostly adjusted to the new normal. I still loathe leaving the house at night. I just want to keep watching the shows we were going through (Holly did not believe in binging, so one episode of anything per day), keep the routine going.
Shortly before she fell ill, I'd asked Holly for a lighter for my birthday to be engraved with my initials. She'd organised it way ahead of schedule, but didn't get a chance to have it engraved. Holly's carers offered to engrave it for me, so I asked them to add her misquoted Alice in Wonderland tattoo. She really Britta'd that one, but it just makes it more special for those of us who recognise the difference. (The quote is supposed to start BEGIN at the beginning... but Holly's hasty Googling led to her downfall). I've kept this on me since it came back, and it's a lovely reminder of her whenever I need a pick me up (or a good cry).
Holly Warland was a Bad Bitch and a powerful witch. Not because she was disabled, but in spite of it. Removed from it. On her own merits, not measured against some bar of lowered expectations. She was smart, funny, and driven. She was confident and fearless, and her legacy will live on in the lives she touched while she was here. Her work advocating for disability recognition and Voluntary Assisted Dying (Australia's euthanasia laws) are some of her standouts, and while she fell just short of completing her PhD, she'd found some modicum of fulfilment in being a life partner and cat mother. Nothing I write in a single post will ever capture Holly's entirety, but I will always think of her lovingly and take the experiences and lessons during our time together with me wherever I end up.
Cat Tax. From left to right: Fat Whiskey, Teddy Bear, Raggy Daggy. They're doing okay. They were confused and lonely for a while, but we're all in sync now. I want to give an enormous, heart-felt thanks to everyone who ever upvoted, followed, commented, donated, Patreoned, or otherwise supported Holly and myself during our time together. You helped make her life measurably better and easier. I'm posting updates very occasionally on her hollywiththemd instagram account, but I'll make an effort to cross post here in future. Please hug your loved ones extra tight for both of us. - Luke "I have realized that the moon did not have to be full for us to love it, that we are not tragedies stranded here beneath it, that if my heart really broke every time I fell from love I’d be able to offer you confetti by now." - Buddy Wakefield
item.dc:creator: PhallecBaldwin

item.title: This inspector exposed poor building practices. A national builder and AZ BTR board has a meeting to remove his licence.
item.description: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/i5nuY-IAdIs At the end they couldn't find any specific violation, so they issued the inspector a letter of concern. Full meeting https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dKq-P_4UynU This is America.

item.title: Australians' right to disconnect
item.description: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/LsGYcuP6rLo A new law granting Australians the “right to disconnect” went into effect on Monday. This means many Australians can now ignore emails, messages or calls after normal working hours. Their employer could be fined if they violate this rule.
item.dc:creator: GlobalImageServiceCat

item.title: Saturday AM meme dump-a-roo
item.description:
I second that tremendous idea
Spent a few hours playing this gem
Tazmanians got jokes
Hypocrits abound
Hard pass
Every time I eat jelly beans, I think about Reagan. Every. Single. Time.
Heading over to the ol' emulator/rom website to download this gem
Fixed that for you
"You will not evade me!"
Texas Roadhouse rolls and cinnamon butter rent space in my head
Cat tax

item.title: Matthew and Johnny Gaudreau died last night
item.description: This will probably get buried in usersub (and perhaps someone else has posted) but two young men died last night in NJ. They were riding their bikes and an alleged drunk driver plowed into them while trying to pass on the outside. What a tragic day for this family, those two men were in New Jersey to see their sister get married. Matthew leaves behind a pregnant wife and Johnny leaves behind a wife and 2 small children, one just born this year. An unimaginable tragedy. Please don’t drink and drive. https://www.cbsnews.com/amp/philadelphia/news/johnny-gaudreau-accident-blue-jackets-new-jersey/
item.dc:creator: PFCHudson

item.title: They don't want me in their life, they want someone else that I'm not.
item.description:
I came to the conclusion a long time ago that if my parents want me in their life, they're going to want ME in their life, not this made up fake person they think I am. I'm not their son. I never was, and I never will be. I only ever pretended because I had no choice. Now that I'm living on my own and I'm self sufficient, I don't need them, like at all. I've gotten used to life without them in my life, and I'm much happier for it.
meme template if anyone wants to use it
item.dc:creator: BerryBnuuy

item.title: It's me, Bottom Shelf.
item.description:
@HileyNoteson Yoooooooooooooooooooooo
Uncensored photo in the comments.
Join for the games. Shit, man. We play everything...if there's a specific game you like, I guarantee you'll find a shitposting meme loving degen there to play it with you. Join for the memes. We share memes in the meme dumpster 24/7 Join for the NSFW memes. Join to ping @humlyhums Join to meet @crimebrulee the biggest problem in the server. discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka
twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka Come heckle us. Follow the channel to be notified when we go live.
Deaf cat Magoo (short for Oogala Magoogala) tax.
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.title: Assorted Fruits of Wrath
item.description: I have been looking for this one forever. Like so many other comic sites, her archive is a mess. Anyway, posted for the purpose of memery.
